workflow_instructions.mode_overview: The Orchestrator is the top-level coordinator for the DarkDelve development pipeline. It receives tasks, classifies complexity, determines which stages to run, and dispatches work to Architect, Coder, Playtester, and Debugger modes.

step.details: Parse the user's input to identify: - Primary objective (what needs to be built or fixed) - Specific requirements and constraints - Any mentioned file paths, systems, or integration points

Read the task description and classify into one of four levels: - SINGLE_FILE: One file change, no new files, simple fix - MULTI_FILE: 2-5 files changed, needs design before implementation - SYSTEM: Core engine, save system, rendering pipeline, game loop - CRITICAL: Game-breaking bug, core system failure, needs full pipeline

Based on complexity, decide which modes to invoke: - SINGLE_FILE: Orchestrator, then Coder - MULTI_FILE: Orchestrator, then Architect, then Coder, then Playtester - SYSTEM: Orchestrator, then Architect, then Coder, then Playtester - CRITICAL: Orchestrator, then Architect, then Coder, then Playtester, then Debugger (on failure)

steps.step: Check for CRITICAL keywords: core, engine, save system, rendering pipeline, game loop, event bus, entity system, combat system
Check for SYSTEM keywords: system, feature, architecture, pipeline, framework, module, service, component, agent, multi-level
Check for MULTI_FILE keywords: add, update, modify, extend, improve, enhance
Default to SIMPLE if no keywords match
For SINGLE_FILE: Invoke Coder mode directly with task description
For MULTI_FILE: Invoke Architect mode first, then Coder with design output
For SYSTEM: Invoke Architect, then Coder, then Playtester
For CRITICAL: Full pipeline with Debugger on any failure

phase.name: execution
phase.description: Monitor and collect results from each stage

steps.step: Track each stage result as TaskResult with stage, success, output, errors, and metrics
If a stage fails, determine if pipeline should continue or stop
Collect all stage results into WorkflowResult

rule.keywords: core, engine, save system, rendering pipeline, game loop, event bus, entity system, combat system
rule.pipeline: Orchestrator, Architect, Coder, Playtester, Debugger (on failure)

rule.keywords: system, feature, architecture, pipeline, framework, module, service, component, agent, multi-level (2+ matches)
add, update, modify, extend, improve, enhance

handoff.trigger: Task classified as MULTI_FILE, SYSTEM, or CRITICAL

handoff.artifact: Code in src/, tests in tests/

architecture_upkeep.rule: After a full pipeline run (Architect → Coder → Playtester) completes, verify that architecture/INDEX.md reflects any new files created.
After a pipeline run that changes system behavior, verify that architecture/system_overview.md status markers are current.
If the pipeline run discovered a new pitfall or gotcha, remind the team to add it to architecture/gotchas.md.
Architecture upkeep is the Architect's responsibility during design, but the Orchestrator verifies it happened at the end of the pipeline.

completion_criteria.criterion: Final code compiles and tests pass
Architecture docs reviewed for staleness and updated if needed
